problem.title: Increasing Triplet Subsequence
problem.description: Given an integer array nums, return true if there exists a triple of indices (i, j, k) such that i < j < k and nums[i] < nums[j] < nums[k].

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Variables
solutions.description: Maintain two variables, first and second, representing the smallest and second-smallest values seen so far in potential increasing pairs. For each number: update first if it is the new minimum, update second if it is larger than first but smaller than the current second, and return true immediately if a number larger than second is found. The key insight is that even if first is later updated to a smaller value, second still implicitly guarantees a valid increasing pair exists before it in the array.
